text: Bohr's first postulate states that electrons move in:
options: Circular orbits where centripetal force = electrostatic force: \( mv^2/r = ke^2/r^2 \)
explanation: Bohr's 1st postulate: electrons revolve in circular stationary orbits. Centripetal force provided by Coulomb attraction: \( mv^2/r = ke^2/r^2 \).
name: Bohr's First Postulate

options: Electrons can be in any orbit
Angular momentum is quantised: \( mvr = n\hbar = nh/2\pi \)
Speed is constant in all orbits
explanation: Bohr's 2nd postulate: \( L = mvr = n\hbar \) (n = 1, 2, 3...). Quantisation of angular momentum. This is equivalent to de Broglie's condition \( 2\pi r = n\lambda \).
name: Bohr's Quantisation Condition

text: The radius of the nth Bohr orbit of hydrogen is:
explanation: Bohr radius: \( r_n = n^2 a_0 \), where \( a_0 = 0.529\) Å. Ground state (n=1): 0.529 Å. n=2: 2.116 Å.
name: Bohr Orbit Radius

text: The energy of hydrogen atom in the nth orbit is:
explanation: Hydrogen energy levels: \( E_n = -13.6/n^2\,\text{eV} \). Ground state (n=1): -13.6 eV. Negative = bound state. Ionisation energy = 13.6 eV.
name: Hydrogen Energy Levels

text: The Lyman series (hydrogen spectrum) corresponds to transitions to \(n=1\) from:
explanation: Lyman series: \( n_1=1 \), \( n_2 = 2,3,4,... \). Lies in UV. Balmer (\(n_1=2\)): visible. Paschen (\(n_1=3\)): IR.
name: Lyman Series Transitions

text: The wavelength of spectral line from hydrogen when electron jumps from \(n_2\) to \(n_1\) is given by:
name: Rydberg Formula

text: The ionisation energy of hydrogen from the ground state is:
explanation: Ionisation energy: energy to remove electron from n=1 to \(n=\infty\). \( E_{ion} = 0 - (-13.6) = 13.6\,\text{eV} \).
name: Ionisation Energy of Hydrogen

options: Nucleus is very small, dense, and positively charged (most of atom is empty space)
explanation: Rutherford's experiment: most \(\alpha\) particles passed through, few deflected at large angles → small, dense, positive nucleus. Disproved Thomson's model.
name: Rutherford Experiment Conclusion

text: The speed of electron in the nth Bohr orbit is:
explanation: \( v_n = e^2/(2\epsilon_0 nh) = v_1/n \), where \( v_1 = c/137 \approx 2.18\times10^6\,\text{m/s} \). Speed decreases with \(n\).
name: Electron Speed in Bohr Orbit

text: The frequency of radiation emitted when electron jumps from \(n_2\) to \(n_1\) is:
explanation: Bohr's frequency rule: \( h\nu = E_{n_2} - E_{n_1} \) (energy of emitted photon). For emission: \( n_2 > n_1 \Rightarrow \nu > 0 \).
name: Bohr's Frequency Rule

text: The total number of spectral lines possible from the nth excited state is:
explanation: Number of lines from level \(n\): \( ^nC_2 = n(n-1)/2 \). From n=4: \( 4\times3/2 = 6 \) lines possible.
name: Number of Spectral Lines

text: de Broglie's explanation of Bohr's quantisation: the condition \( mvr = n\hbar \) is equivalent to:
options: The orbit must be an integer multiple of radius
The circumference must contain integer number of de Broglie wavelengths: \( 2\pi r = n\lambda \)
explanation: de Broglie: \( \lambda = h/mv \). For standing wave in orbit: \( 2\pi r = n\lambda = nh/mv \Rightarrow mvr = nh/2\pi \). Beautifully explains Bohr's ad hoc condition.
name: de Broglie and Bohr's Quantisation

options: Spectra of multi-electron atoms or fine structure of spectral lines
explanation: Bohr model fails for: multi-electron atoms, fine structure (splitting), Stark and Zeeman effects, relative intensities of spectral lines. Only works for H-like atoms.
name: Limitations of Bohr Model

text: The electron in hydrogen at n=2 has energy:
name: Energy at n=2

text: Excitation energy of hydrogen from n=1 to n=2 is:
explanation: \( \Delta E = E_2 - E_1 = -3.4 - (-13.6) = 10.2\,\text{eV} \). This is the minimum energy needed to excite hydrogen from ground state.
name: Excitation Energy n=1 to n=2
